content: Sepsis is defined (Sepsis-3, 2016) as life-threatening organ dysfunction caused by a dysregulated host response to infection. Septic shock is a subset of sepsis with circulatory and cellular/metabolic abnormalities associated with higher mortality: persistent hypotension requiring vasopressors to maintain MAP ≥65 mmHg AND serum lactate >2 mmol/L despite adequate fluid resuscitation. Organ dysfunction is identified by acute change in SOFA score ≥2 points. Quick SOFA (qSOFA) uses respiratory rate ≥22/min, altered mentation, or systolic BP ≤100 mmHg to identify patients with suspected infection at risk for poor outcomes.
